clinicalFoundation.population: A child starting school who does not know where the bathroom is and feels their body tighten when they need to go.
clinicalFoundation.trigger: Long hallways and unfamiliar doors during the first week.

therapeuticIntention.feel: brave enough to ask a safe adult
therapeuticIntention.because: Asking once can make the scary place known and the body can relax a little.

clinicalFoundation.creativeVision: A hallway drawn like a gentle maze with one door that glows a little softer than the rest.
clinicalFoundation.oneTrueThing: The office secretary has a sticker bowl for brave questions.

typeSpecificField.freeText: Has to wiggle toes to stay standing in line.

mustNeverList: No adult ridicule or shame for needing the bathroom
